zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes van Loon於大約1143年出生在比利時利包格波格洛時，她的父親Lodewijk I van Loon35歲，母親Agnes de Metz30歲。她於1169年與Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 凱拉海姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1191年3月26日在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 蒂賓根享年49歲，埋葬在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 普法分霍分 安 德 易爾姆也。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes van Loon于大约1143年出生在比利时利包格波格洛时，她的父亲Lodewijk I van Loon35岁，母亲Agnes de Metz30岁。她于1169年与Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 凱拉海姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1191年3月26日在德国巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 蒂賓根享年49岁，埋葬在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 普法分霍分 安 德 易爾姆也。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Loon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lon .
possibly a variant of English Lund , which is either a topographic name from Old Norse lundr ‘grove’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word.

variants.name: Lon
meanings: English: probably a variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lown .
Cambodian: written លន់, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘(to be) superb’. Compare   Lun   4.

Burmese (Shan): from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, of unexplained etymology. Compare   Lone   4. — Note: Since Shans do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Lundy
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places called Lundie, for example one near Doune in Perthshire.
Irish: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French de la Launde ‘of the glade’. Compare Lown 1.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes van Loon was born about 1143, in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium, her father, Lodewijk I van Loon, was 35 and her mother, Agnes de Metz, was 30. She married Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern in 1169, in Kelheim, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 26 March 1191, in Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 49, and was buried in Scheyern, Pfaffenhofen, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes van Loon est née environ 1143, à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique, son père, Lodewijk I van Loon, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Metz, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern en 1169, à Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 mars 1191, à Landkreis Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Scheyern, Landkreis Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes van Loon um 1143 in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lodewijk I van Loon 35 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Metz 30. Sie hat 1169 in Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Deutschland Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 26. März 1191 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Landkreis Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland und wurde in Scheyern, Landkreis Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes van Loon è nata il circa 1143 nel Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio, sua padre, Lodewijk I van Loon, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Metz, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern nel 1169, nel Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 26 marzo 1191, nel Landkreis Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Scheyern, Landkreis Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm, Bayern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes van Loonが約1143年にベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンで生まれた時，父Lodewijk I van Loonは35歳，母Agnes de Metzは30歳であった。1169年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ケルヘイムでOtto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayernと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1191年3月26日にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグランドクレイス トuビンジェンで49歳で死去し，ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス プファフエンhオフン アン ダー イルムシェイエルンにて埋葬された。

ko.Scheyern, Pfaffenhofen, Bavaria, Germany: 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 파아펜호펜 앤 데르 이름 슈예른
ko.lifeSummary: Agnes van Loon(이)가 대략 1143년 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lodewijk I van Loon(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Metz(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1169년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 켈하임에서 Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1191년 3월 26일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 트u빙에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 파아펜호펜 앤 데르 이름 슈예른에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes van Loon nasceu aproximadamente 1143, em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Lodewijk I van Loon, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Metz, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern em 1169, em Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 26 de março de 1191, em Landkreis Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Scheyern, Landkreis Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.Scheyern, Pfaffenhofen, Bavaria, Germany: щиирн, ландкрис пфаффенофен ан дир илм, беиирн, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes van Loon родилась около 1143 г. в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Lodewijk I van Loon было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Metz, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern в 1169 г. в местности ландкрис келхим, беиирн, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 26 марта 1191 г. в местности ландкрис тuбинген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности щиирн, ландкрис пфаффенофен ан дир илм, беиирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes van Loon nació aproximadamente 1143, en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Lodewijk I van Loon, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Agnes de Metz, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern en 1169, en Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 26 de marzo de 1191, en Landkreis Tübingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultada en Scheyern, Landkreis Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm, Bayern, Alemania.
